title: Censored time-to-next-stop model as an auxiliary specialist
summary: XGBoost AFT is built for censored time-to-event targets.
rationale: XGBoost AFT is built for censored time-to-event targets. For pit timing, many laps are naturally right-censored because the stop has not happened yet by sequence end, so survival-style supervision is a cleaner fit than only binary next-lap labels.
implementation_hint: Create per-row target bounds for laps until next pit for the same Race-Year-Driver: exact [k,k] when a future stop exists, right-censored [k,+inf) when none remains. Train an AFT model and convert its implied survival/hazard near t=1 into a next-lap probability feature or blend member.
expected_effect: Should add a smoother notion of stop imminence and help separate “not next lap but soon” from “not stopping for a long time.”
risk: Target construction must be done only on training folds and may be noisy around race-end or weather-driven stops; converting AFT output to a calibrated next-lap probability may need careful OOF mapping.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
